channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Democrats strike deal to get more Biden judges confirmed before Congress adjourns
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate won't hold votes on four of President Joe Biden's appellate court nominees as part of a deal with Republicans to allow for speedier consideration of other judicial nominations and bring Biden within striking distance of the 234 total judicial confirmations that occurred
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Pentagon 'fundamentally rejects' ICC decision to issue arrest warrant for Netanyahu
item.description: The Pentagon rejected the International Criminal Court's decision to issue arrest warrants for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and former Defense Minister Yoav Gallant.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pathologist disputes finding that Marine veteran's chokehold caused subway rider's death
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — For roughly six minutes, Jordan Neely was pinned to a subway floor in a chokehold that ended with him lying still. But that's not what killed him, a forensic pathologist testified Thursday in defense of the military-trained commuter charged with killing Neely. Dr.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Death toll in Gaza from Israel-Hamas war passes 44,000, Palestinian officials say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The death toll in the Gaza Strip from the 13-month-old war between Israel and Hamas has surpassed 44,000, local health officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 3 Utah children missing since 2022 found living with FLDS Church, returned to mother
item.description: Three Utah children missing for two years have been located living with members of the Fundamentalist Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints, according to the Fredonia Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, ABC 4

item.title: Passengers duct tape unruly man who tried to open door on American Airlines plane
item.description: A group of passengers aboard a Dallas-bound flight restrained a man with duct tape after he claimed to be the "captain" and attempted to open the cabin door of an American Airlines plane midair, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump chooses Pam Bondi for attorney general pick after Gaetz withdraws
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Thursday named Pam Bondi, the former attorney general of Florida, to be U.S. attorney general just hours after his other choice, Matt Gaetz, withdrew his name.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Democrats will push for release of Gaetz report despite withdrawal from AG running
item.description: Rep. Sean Casten (D-Ill.), who had introduced a resolution on releasing the report, said the public still has the right to know.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Trump has promised again to release the last JFK files. But experts say don’t expect big revelations
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — More than 60 years after President John F. Kennedy was assassinated, conspiracy theories still swirl and any new glimpse into the fateful day of Nov. 22, 1963, in Dallas continues to fascinate.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gaetz withdrawal from AG nomination is among quickest in history
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) withdrew his nomination as President-elect Trump’s attorney general on Thursday, putting an end to a chaotic several days on Capitol Hill and making him one of the quickest in history to withdraw from Cabinet nominations.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Historically bad year for dolphin strandings on Cape Cod has scientists looking for answers
item.description: An unprecedentedly bad year for beached dolphins on Cape Cod might have to do with warming waters changing the availability of the animals' food, said scientists hoping to curb the strandings.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 73% worry Social Security won't pay promised benefits: Survey
item.description: Most nonretired U.S. adults, 73%, are worried they won't get their promised Social Security benefits when they retire.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Police report reveals assault allegations against Hegseth, Trump's pick for defense secretary
item.description: SANTA CRUZ, Calif. (AP) — A woman told police that she was sexually assaulted in 2017 by Pete Hegseth after he took her phone, blocked the door to a California hotel room and refused to let her leave, according to a detailed investigative report made public late Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA MENDOZA, BRIAN SLODYSKO and JULIET LINDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about a storm bringing high winds, heavy rain, snow to California and Pacific Northwest
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — One of the strongest storms on the West Coast in decades knocked out power for thousands of people, unleashed strong winds that toppled trees and left two dead in Washington before making its way through Oregon to Northern California where on Thursday it dropped heavy snow and r
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A kayaker faked his own death and fled overseas. Now he's telling how he did it
item.description: GREEN LAKE, Wis. (AP) — A Wisconsin man who faked his own drowning this summer so he could abandon his wife and three children has been communicating with authorities daily from Eastern Europe, even telling them how he did it, but has not committed to returning home, a sheriff said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Thanksgiving meal deals: Here's what grocery stores are offering
item.description: Major grocers like Walmart and Target are offering "inflation-free" Thanksgiving meal deals. Here's what you can buy.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Attorneys want the US Supreme Court to say Mississippi's felony voting ban is cruel and unusual
item.description: JACKSON, Miss. (AP) — The U.S. Supreme Court should overturn Mississippi's Jim Crow-era practice of removing voting rights from people convicted of certain felonies, including nonviolent crimes such as forgery and timber theft, attorneys say in new court papers.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Weird laws in Massachusetts including a $20 fine, possible jail time for frightening a pigeon
item.description: In Massachusetts, be cautious before dyeing a chick, defacing a milk carton or scaring a pigeon, as these acts are all illegal in the state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Meghan Markle's bad week as Prince Harry outshines his wife
item.description: Across the pond in America, former British royal, Prince Harry, is making inroads to make himself likable again… and it’s working.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Experts are expecting the busiest Thanksgiving travel season ever
item.description: Nearly 80 million Americans will travel more than 50 miles for the upcoming Thanksgiving holiday, according to AAA, surpassing even pre-pandemic levels. That estimate would be an increase of 1.7 million people compared to last year and 2 million more than in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: St. Louis was once known as Mound City for its many Native American mounds. Just one remains
item.description: ST. LOUIS (AP) — What is now St. Louis was once home to more than 100 mounds constructed by Native Americans — so many that St. Louis was once known as “Mound City.” Settlers tore most of them down, and just one remains.
item.dc:creator: JIM SALTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Critics accuse Georgia sheriff of silencing them on social media in lawsuit
item.description: Social media critics of Cobb County Sheriff Craig Owens are accusing him in a lawsuit of violating their free speech rights on Facebook.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Assault allegations against Hegseth revealed in police report
item.description: A woman told police that she was sexually assaulted in 2017 by Pete Hegseth after he took her phone, blocked the door to a California hotel room and refused to let her leave, according to a detailed investigative report made public late Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man wins $650K on lottery ticket using numbers tied to late wife
item.description: A Chicago man won a $650,000 Lucky Day Lotto jackpot prize after playing numbers related to his late wife.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, ABC 4

item.title: Florist gets death threats after refusing to serve Trump voters
item.description: The owner posted on social media that the business is now temporarily closed after receiving several death threats. She also made all of her social media accounts private.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Knox, NewsNation

item.title: Attack of the trees: Aging giants wreak havoc in Missouri neighborhood
item.description: "We’re pretty concerned. We’re thinking, ‘Should we move before the trees destroy our home?’” said one resident.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, KRON4

item.title: Judge keeps death penalty a possibility for man charged in killings of 4 Idaho students
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — The death penalty will remain a possibility for a man charged with murder in the stabbing deaths of four University of Idaho students, a judge ruled Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NYC tourist stabbed in swanky area after 3 killed in random attack as DA slams Trump
item.description: A 55-year-old tourist was slashed in his face on the Upper West Side in New York City in another unprovoked street stabbing, police confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Delta to begin serving Shake Shack on select flights
item.description: Travelers soon will be able to enjoy Shake Shack's famous burgers on select Delta flights beginning next month, with plans to expand throughout the U.S. in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WGN 9

item.title: Climate change goosed hurricane wind strength by 18 mph since 2019, study says
item.description: BAKU, Azerbaijan (AP) — Human-caused climate change made Atlantic hurricanes about 18 miles per hour (29 kilometers per hour) stronger in the last six years, a new scientific study found Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hacker gains access to testimony in Gaetz investigations: reports
item.description: A hacker gained access to witness testimony related to investigations into former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.), who resigned from Congress last week after President-elect Trump tapped him for attorney general, multiple news outlets reported Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Regina Zilbermints, Fox 5
